relevant_files.rationale: Core init command — calls initFabric() which creates .fabric/bootstrap/README.md, agents.meta.json, human-lock.json, forensic.json, plus Claude skill/hook/settings; also orchestrates installBootstrap, installMcpClients, installHooks stages with per-stage result tracking

key_code.description: Creates all .fabric/ core files and optional .claude/ skill/hook files; returns per-file action records (created|overwritten)
Idempotently merges the agents-md-init-reminder.cjs stop hook into .claude/settings.json with atomic rename write
Builds the initial agents.meta.json structure with a single L0 node pointing to .fabric/bootstrap/README.md
Renders a CLI table showing bootstrap/mcp/hook/skill capabilities per detected AI client

relevant_files.topic_relation: Init flow entry point — understanding this file is prerequisite to any optimization of the AI client initialization rules and bootstrap protocol

relevant_files.rationale: Implements installBootstrap() called by init.ts; maps ClientKind to bootstrap target paths and delegates to ensureFabricBootstrapGuide; currently marks all targets as 'skipped' meaning bootstrap writing is now centralized in initFabric

key_code.description: Resolves bootstrap targets from fabricConfig + resolveClients(), calls ensureFabricBootstrapGuide, currently returns all as skipped
Maps CLI shorthand strings (claude-code, roo-code, etc.) to canonical BootstrapClient types for --clients arg parsing

relevant_files.topic_relation: Bootstrap protocol installation — relevant to understanding why bootstrap stage shows 'skipped' and how client-specific bootstrap targets are resolved

relevant_files.rationale: Generates .fabric/bootstrap/README.md content by detecting framework, selecting framework-specific AGENTS.md template (cocos/vite/next or generic), and substituting project name

key_code.description: Loads framework-specific AGENTS.md template, replaces { projectName } and { frameworkKind } tokens, returns content
Maps framework kind to template variant path for cocos-creator, vite, next

relevant_files.topic_relation: Controls what AI clients see in their L0 bootstrap context — directly shapes the initialization rules served to all AI clients

relevant_files.rationale: Detects which AI clients are present (Claude, Cursor, Windsurf, Roo, Gemini, Codex) by probing ~/.claude, ~/.gemini, .cursor/, .windsurf/, .roo/ directories; defines capability matrix per client (bootstrap/mcp/hook/skill)

key_code.description: Returns DetectedClientSupport[] with capabilities flags per client — only ClaudeCodeCLI has all 4 capabilities; others lack hook/skill
Returns ClientConfigWriter[] for MCP config installation, probing homedir and workspace for client-specific directories

relevant_files.topic_relation: Client detection determines which init stages are active; capability matrix governs what's installed per AI client — central to optimization of per-client init behavior

relevant_files.rationale: Generates .fabric/forensic.json with topology analysis, entry point detection, code samples, pattern assertions, and candidate files; used by init.ts during fab init to snapshot project state for AI clients

key_code.description: Orchestrates full forensic analysis: topology walk, entry point collection, code sampling, assertion building, candidate file selection
Detects cocos-component-class, react-root, next-route-component, vite-main-entry, source-entry patterns with co-occurrence evidence
Builds 6 structured assertions (framework, dominant-pattern, entry-directory, meta-sidecar, config, domain) with confidence and coverage

relevant_files.topic_relation: Forensic snapshot feeds AI client context during init; richer forensic data = better AI client understanding of the project at initialization time

relevant_files.rationale: Core fab_get_rules service — resolves L0/L1/L2 rules for a target file path using minimatch against nodes in agents.meta.json; also loads human_locked_nearby context and appends audit events

key_code.description: Main entry: loads context from cache, checks stale via client_hash comparison, resolves rules for path, appends audit telemetry
Calls loadRulesForPath then partitionRulesByLevel to return L0 string + L1/L2 entry arrays + human_locked_nearby
Filters meta.nodes by minimatch(path, node.scope_glob), sorts by priority then node ID, reads file contents
Reads agents.meta.json + bootstrap README + human-lock entries into a cached GetRulesContext

relevant_files.topic_relation: This is the runtime rule resolution engine AI clients invoke before every file modification — its correctness and performance directly determines AI client behavior quality

relevant_files.rationale: MCP tool registration for fab_get_rules — exposes the get-rules service as an MCP tool with Zod schemas; AI clients call this before modifying any file to receive their rule context

key_code.description: Registers fab_get_rules MCP tool with readOnlyHint annotation; takes path + optional client_hash, returns revision_hash + stale flag + L0/L1/L2/human_locked_nearby

relevant_files.topic_relation: The MCP interface AI clients use for rule retrieval — the contract definition between AI clients and the Fabric rule system

relevant_files.rationale: MCP tool fab_plan_context — batch-queries rules for 2+ file paths in one round-trip; designed for planning/architecture phases to reduce MCP round-trips

key_code.description: Registers fab_plan_context with readOnlyHint; accepts paths array (min 2) + optional client_hash; returns per-path rule entries with stale detection

relevant_files.topic_relation: Batch rule query API for multi-file planning — relevant to optimizing AI client interaction patterns during init and multi-file operations

relevant_files.rationale: MCP tool fab_update_registry — allows AI clients to add/remove/update nodes in agents.meta.json instead of direct file editing; the write-path counterpart to fab_get_rules

key_code.description: Registers fab_update_registry with destructiveHint; supports add-node/remove-node/update-node ops with full node schema; returns success + new revision_hash

relevant_files.topic_relation: AI clients use this to evolve the rule registry during session — understanding this is key to how AI clients can self-update fabric rules

relevant_files.rationale: SSE event system watching .fabric/agents.meta.json, human-lock.json, forensic.json, and .intent-ledger.jsonl; broadcasts meta:updated, drift:detected, lock:drift, lock:approved, ledger:appended events with 50-event ring buffer for missed event replay

key_code.description: Creates SSE handler with chokidar watcher (lazy start on first client), 10-client connection limit, 30s heartbeat, Last-Event-ID resume via ring buffer replay
Dispatches file change to appropriate event factory: meta:updated for agents.meta.json, lock:drift/approved for human-lock.json, drift:detected for forensic.json, ledger:appended for ledger
Computes diff between previous and current HumanLockSnapshot to determine if lock:approved or lock:drift should fire

relevant_files.topic_relation: SSE bridge between filesystem changes and dashboard Web UI — the real-time connection between CLI operations and web state display

relevant_files.rationale: HTTP app factory — registers all REST APIs, SSE /events, MCP /mcp endpoint with session management and JSONL EventStore; also runs persistent chokidar watcher for agents.meta.json changes to push MCP notifications (tools/list_changed, resource_updated) to active sessions

key_code.description: Registers all routes, sets up bearer auth middleware, creates persistent cacheWatcher for agents.meta.json + bootstrap README, manages MCP sessions
Sends tools/list_changed or resource_updated MCP notifications to all active sessions when fabric meta changes
Implements MCP EventStore using .intent-ledger.jsonl — enables MCP session resumability across reconnects

relevant_files.topic_relation: The HTTP bridge layer connecting CLI file changes to both Web dashboard (SSE) and AI clients (MCP notifications) — core architecture for CLI-Web interaction

relevant_files.rationale: Creates the McpServer instance with all 4 tools (fab_get_rules, fab_plan_context, fab_append_intent, fab_update_registry) and the bootstrap README resource; supports both stdio (for direct MCP) and HTTP transports

key_code.description: Builds McpServer with 4 tools + bootstrap README resource at fabric://bootstrap/README.md URI

relevant_files.topic_relation: MCP server configuration — what capabilities AI clients see when connecting; relevant to extending or changing AI client init protocols

relevant_files.rationale: REST endpoint GET /api/rules — returns full agents.meta.json for the dashboard rules tree view; separate from MCP fab_get_rules which resolves rules per path

REST endpoint GET /api/scan — creates real-time scan report with framework detection, readme quality, file counts, and recommendations; used by dashboard and potentially relevant to init status display

key_code.description: Detects framework, checks readme quality, hasExistingFabric flag, walks file tree with DEFAULT_IGNORES, builds recommendations for L0/L1 setup
Generates actionable recommendations: run fab init if no .fabric exists, expand README, add CONTRIBUTING.md, check framework directories for L1 files

relevant_files.topic_relation: Dashboard scan view shows project initialization status — relevant to improving web display of init state

relevant_files.rationale: Reads and validates .fabric/agents.meta.json with Zod schema; uses ContextCache (TTL 5s) for hot-path caching; throws typed errors AgentsMetaFileMissingError and AgentsMetaInvalidError

key_code.description: Reads + validates agents.meta.json with 5s cache; throws AgentsMetaFileMissingError on ENOENT for clear error surfacing

relevant_files.topic_relation: Foundation of rule resolution and event broadcasting — cache invalidation behavior directly impacts rule freshness for AI clients

relevant_files.rationale: ContextCache singleton with TTL-based meta/context slots and invalidation-only audit slot; invalidation reasons: meta_write (eager after service mutations) and file_watch (chokidar-triggered); default TTL 5s

key_code.description: Three-slot cache: metaSlot (AgentsMeta), contextSlot (GetRulesContext), auditSlot (cursor); invalidate() differentiates meta_write vs file_watch scope

relevant_files.topic_relation: Cache invalidation strategy determines rule freshness latency — relevant to init flow performance and consistency between CLI writes and AI client reads

relevant_files.rationale: Dashboard HTTP client — all fetch wrappers (getRules, getDoctor, getScan, getHumanLock, getLedger, getHistoryState) plus SSE connection manager with exponential backoff reconnect and auth token injection from window.__FABRIC_AUTH_TOKEN__ or ?token= URL param

key_code.description: Opens SSE connection using fetch with AbortController; parses text/event-stream blocks; calls onOpen on 200 OK, onClose on disconnect for reconnect scheduling
Reads auth token from window.__FABRIC_AUTH_TOKEN__ or ?token= URL param (removes from URL after reading to avoid sharing in links)
GET /api/rules — fetches full AgentsMeta for dashboard rules tree

relevant_files.topic_relation: Dashboard-to-server communication layer — SSE connection with reconnect logic is the web side of the CLI-Web bridge; auth token injection enables secure remote access

relevant_files.rationale: React hook managing SSE connection lifecycle with exponential backoff (1s→30s×2); tracks last event ID for missed-event resume; maintains global Preact signals for event history (50 events) and connection status

key_code.description: Hook with connect() that opens SSE, handles reconnect with backoff, tracks lastEventIdRef for resume, updates fabricEventsSignal and fabricConnectedSignal
Global Preact signal holding last 50 FabricEvents — shared across components without prop drilling

relevant_files.topic_relation: Real-time event state management in web dashboard — how SSE events from CLI operations propagate to all dashboard views

relevant_files.rationale: Dashboard SPA shell with hash-based routing (#/rules, #/locks, #/timeline, #/history, #/doctor), sidebar nav, connection status badge, and lastEvent prop forwarding to all views

key_code.description: Routes lastEvent from useEvents() to each view component; shows connected/connecting badge with port label; defaults to #/rules on empty hash

relevant_files.topic_relation: Top-level web interaction architecture — how CLI events flow from SSE to all dashboard view components via lastEvent prop

relevant_files.rationale: Rules Tree view displays agents.meta.json node hierarchy with human-lock indicators and drift warnings; auto-refreshes on meta:updated, drift:detected, lock:drift events

key_code.description: Shows rules tree with filter input, refresh button, node count/revision status line; auto-loads on lastEvent types meta:updated/drift:detected/lock:drift
Builds 3-level tree: root (revision) → groups (by first path segment) → leaf nodes (actual meta nodes) with humanLockedNearby and staleReason

relevant_files.topic_relation: Primary dashboard view for rule state visualization — directly shows the result of init and registry update operations

relevant_files.rationale: Doctor view shows framework detection, protected path integrity, ledger activity, and per-check status; auto-refreshes on meta:updated, lock:approved/drift, ledger:appended, drift:detected events

Defines AgentsMeta and AgentsMetaNode Zod schemas; derives layer (L0/L1/L2) and topology_type (mirror/cross-cutting) from file path — AGENTS.md/.fabric/bootstrap/README.md → L0, _cross/ segment → L1/cross-cutting, depth <= 2 → L1, deeper → L2

key_code.description: Layer derivation logic: AGENTS.md + bootstrap README = L0; _cross segment = L1; directory depth 0=L0, 1-2=L1, >2=L2
Zod schema: { revision: string, nodes: Record<string, AgentsMetaNode> }

relevant_files.topic_relation: The schema that governs how rules are classified into L0/L1/L2 layers — fundamental to understanding initialization rules architecture

relevant_files.rationale: CLI scan command; also exports createScanReport() used by bootstrap-guide.ts to detect framework during init — the shared scan logic between CLI and server
Implements installHooks() for git hooks (pre-commit, lefthook) — the third stage of fab init; installs human-lint hooks to enforce protected file change discipline

Generic AGENTS.md template used as fallback during buildFabricBootstrapGuide() when no framework-specific variant exists; defines the L0 bootstrap content structure with { projectName } and { frameworkKind } tokens
Template for .fabric/human-lock.json created during fab init; defines the initial empty human-lock structure that the server monitors for drift detection

existing_patterns.name: L0/L1/L2 rule layering
existing_patterns.description: Rules are layered by specificity: L0 = global bootstrap (AGENTS.md / .fabric/bootstrap/README.md), L1 = cross-cutting or shallow-depth rules (.fabric/agents/*.md depth <= 2), L2 = deep scoped rules (depth > 2). Layer is derived from file path, not stored redundantly. AI clients receive all matching layers in one getRules() call.

existing_patterns.name: MCP tool convention
existing_patterns.description: All MCP tools follow the pattern: registerXxx(server) → server.registerTool(name, {description, inputSchema, outputSchema, annotations}, handler). Handler resolves projectRoot via FABRIC_PROJECT_ROOT env var, calls service function, returns both text and structuredContent.

existing_patterns.name: SSE event-driven dashboard refresh
existing_patterns.description: Dashboard views receive lastEvent prop from useEvents() hook. Each view has a useEffect that triggers load() on specific event types. Views do NOT poll; they refresh reactively. Missed events during disconnection are replayed via Last-Event-ID ring buffer (50 events, BACKOFF 1s→30s).

existing_patterns.name: Context cache with dual invalidation
existing_patterns.description: ContextCache has TTL-based slots (5s default) for meta and context; additionally supports eager invalidation via invalidate('meta_write') after service mutations and invalidate('file_watch') after chokidar detects disk changes. Cache is a module singleton.

existing_patterns.name: Init stage tracking with skip/ran/failed disposition
existing_patterns.description: fab init tracks each stage (bootstrap, mcp, hooks) as InitStageRecord with disposition: ran|skipped|failed. Capability summary table shows per-client results. Stage failures don't abort subsequent stages — all run independently.

existing_patterns.name: Forensic report as project snapshot
existing_patterns.description: ForensicReport captures topology (file counts/extensions/key dirs), entry points, code samples with pattern hints, and structured assertions with confidence/coverage. Written to .fabric/forensic.json at init time. Server watches this file and broadcasts drift:detected on change.

existing_patterns.name: Auth token injection for dashboard
existing_patterns.description: Dashboard reads auth token from window.__FABRIC_AUTH_TOKEN__ (server-injected) or ?token= URL query param (removes from URL after reading). Token is added as Authorization: Bearer header on all API and SSE requests.

existing_patterns.name: Client detection via filesystem probes
existing_patterns.description: AI client detection uses existsSync on known config directories (~/.claude, .cursor/, .windsurf/, .roo/, ~/.gemini, ~/.codex) and config files. Only ClaudeCodeCLI has all 4 capabilities (bootstrap+mcp+hook+skill). Other clients support bootstrap+mcp only.

architecture_constraints.constraint: Rule files must be in .fabric/ or reference AGENTS.md at project root — layer derivation is path-based
architecture_constraints.impact: Any new rule type must follow the .fabric/agents/<path> pattern or use _cross segment for cross-cutting rules; custom paths outside .fabric/ get L0 or L2 classification based on depth

architecture_constraints.constraint: MCP server is stateless between calls — projectRoot resolved via FABRIC_PROJECT_ROOT env var
architecture_constraints.impact: Cannot maintain per-session state in MCP tools; all context must be read from disk (with caching) on each call

architecture_constraints.constraint: SSE event system is lazy (starts watcher only on first client connection, stops when all disconnect)
architecture_constraints.impact: There is a brief window after the first client connects where missed events between server start and connection are not replayed beyond the ring buffer

architecture_constraints.constraint: Bootstrap stage in init currently marks all targets as 'skipped' — bootstrap content is always written by initFabric()
architecture_constraints.impact: The bootstrap stage separation from initFabric is vestigial; adding per-client bootstrap content differentiation would require refactoring

architecture_constraints.constraint: agents.meta.json revision is a SHA-256 of the bootstrap README hash — changes to the file invalidate all client_hash staleness checks
architecture_constraints.impact: Any modification to bootstrap/README.md cascades to all clients needing rule refresh; batch updates should be atomic

architecture_constraints.constraint: Dashboard is a single-page Preact app with hash-based routing — no server-side rendering
architecture_constraints.impact: Deep linking to specific views works via URL hash; cannot use server-side route handling; all routing is client-side

integration_points.name: fab init → .fabric/ filesystem
integration_points.description: fab init writes .fabric/bootstrap/README.md, .fabric/agents.meta.json, .fabric/human-lock.json, .fabric/forensic.json atomically. Server's chokidar watcher detects these changes and invalidates cache + broadcasts events.

integration_points.name: MCP AI clients ↔ fab_get_rules
integration_points.description: AI clients call fab_get_rules(path, client_hash?) via MCP. Server returns {revision_hash, stale, rules: {L0, L1[], L2[], human_locked_nearby[]}}. client_hash enables client-side stale detection without server round-trip for unchanged rules.

integration_points.name: Server ↔ Dashboard via SSE /events
integration_points.description: Dashboard opens fetch-based SSE connection to /events. Server uses chokidar to watch 4 fabric files and broadcasts typed events. Dashboard useEvents() hook handles reconnect with exponential backoff and Last-Event-ID replay.

integration_points.name: fab init → Claude .claude/settings.json
integration_points.description: fab init merges a Stop hook entry into .claude/settings.json (agents-md-init-reminder.cjs), copies SKILL.md to .claude/skills/, and copies the hook script. This is done atomically with rename.

integration_points.name: Server cache ↔ file watch invalidation
integration_points.description: Two independent watchers: (1) http.ts persistent watcher clears ContextCache on agents.meta.json/bootstrap README changes and sends MCP notifications; (2) events.ts lazy watcher is for SSE broadcasting only.

observations: The bootstrap stage in init.ts is currently a no-op (all skipped) — installBootstrap() delegates to ensureFabricBootstrapGuide() which does nothing if the file already exists. The actual write happens inside initFabric(). This separation could be leveraged to add client-specific bootstrap variants.
detectClientSupports() runs filesystem probes synchronously — for slow network-mounted homes this could be a bottleneck. Detection could be parallelized or cached between init stages.

Init writes 4 core files sequentially — these could be written in parallel since they have no inter-dependencies (forensicReport, bootstrapContent, meta, humanLockTemplate are all computed before any writes).
The Claude skill/hook installation is done in initFabric() but Cursor/Windsurf/Roo have no equivalent — expanding per-client initialization artifacts could enable richer non-Claude clients.

Rule resolution uses minimatch for glob matching but doesn't cache the compiled patterns — for projects with many nodes, compiling patterns on every fab_get_rules call is wasteful. Patterns could be pre-compiled and cached in GetRulesContext.
The stale check (client_hash !== context.meta.revision) is binary — either fresh or stale. A more granular approach could tell clients which specific L1/L2 files changed.
Human lock context is included in every getRules() response regardless of whether any locked files match the requested path — this adds payload size unnecessarily.

Dashboard has no init status view — there is no view showing whether fab init has been run, what the forensic report says, or scan recommendations. Adding a dedicated 'Setup / Init Status' view would improve discoverability.
The scan API (/api/scan) provides hasExistingFabric + recommendations but is not surfaced in the dashboard navigation — only available via API call. The Doctor view partially covers this gap.
Authentication token is consumed from URL ?token= and stripped — this is a clean UX pattern that could be extended with token expiry or auth state visualization in the header.

The SSE ring buffer holds 50 events and uses Last-Event-ID for replay — this means a dashboard that was offline for more than 50 events loses historical context. A longer or persistent event log could improve recovery.

MCP notifications (tools/list_changed, resource_updated) are sent when agents.meta.json changes via the persistent cacheWatcher — this means connected AI clients automatically get notified when another session updates the registry via fab_update_registry.

The JsonlEventStore stores MCP events in .intent-ledger.jsonl alongside application events — this co-mingling means MCP session resumability and audit logging share the same file with kind:mcp-event filtering to separate them.
